entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cicer arietinum, the crop studied in the experiments

entities.name: LENGTH-TO-WEIGHT RATIO
entities.description: A measure of root morphology (LWR) used to assess root growth and response to soil conditions

entities.name: VERTISOL
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil used in the study

entities.name: PATANCHERU
entities.description: The specific site near which experiments were conducted

entities.name: ANDHRA PRADESH
entities.description: The state in India where the study took place

entities.name: DECREASING SOIL MOISTURE
entities.description: The environmental condition of progressively receding soil moisture

entities.name: MONOLITH METHOD
entities.description: The sampling technique used to extract roots at 14-day intervals

entities.name: OXYGEN DEFICIENCY
entities.description: A state caused by excessive soil water status affecting root growth

entities.name: POOR SOIL AERATION
entities.description: A condition in Vertisols that leads to decreased root length-to-weight ratio

entities.name: ROOT MORPHOLOGY
entities.description: The study of the physical form and structure of roots

relationships.description: The trait measured in the chickpea genotypes
The crop was grown in Vertisol soil
The crop was subjected to decreasing soil moisture conditions
The method used to sample roots for calculating the length-to-weight ratio
Available soil moisture has a negative relationship with LWR in the lower rooting zone
Oxygen deficiency caused by excessive water affects the LWR
Poor soil aeration decreased the LWR in Vertisols
Poor soil aeration is a condition observed in Vertisols
Patancheru is located in Andhra Pradesh
LWR is an indicator of root morphology
